bikes.year_notes: Launched Nov 1, 2022 (Electrek 2022-11-01), sold through 2024 with clearance into early 2025; replaced by the XP4 in 2025. ONE verified running change: May 16, 2023 Lectric switched production to Star Union hydraulic disc brakes with 180mm rotors, up from mechanical calipers on 160mm rotors (Electrek 2023-05-16; PRNewswire; Lectric sells the retrofit kit on its site and offered it FREE to existing owners, so many 2022-2023 frames now carry hydraulics). Motor, battery options, and backlit LCD display were unchanged across the whole run.
bikes.blurb: The best selling budget folder in America from 2022 to 2024, replaced by the XP4 in 2025. Owners swap the same four parts in month one: saddle, seatpost, pedals, tires. Takeoff supply is deep and buyer demand from budget repairers is steady.

stock_parts.comp_note: Takeoff stock ebike saddles list $10-25 on eBay and forum classifieds; no set of 3+ confirmed solds found, so priced at the low end. Unchanged across the 2022-2024 run.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: First thing off the bike. The stock pad is thin and everyone feels it by mile five.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used; value is mostly in bundles. Priced accordingly. Unchanged across the run.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: The number one XP comfort fix on every owner forum. Rigid frame plus fat tires still beats you up without it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Folding takeoff pedals sell in the $10-15 range when they sell at all. No confirmed sold set. Unchanged across the run.

upgrade.name: Chester composite platform pedals
upgrade.note: Stock folders are small and slick when wet. A real platform pedal fixes footing for cheap.

stock_parts.comp_note: Used 20x3 takeoff pairs list around $30-40; single tires priced low. Lectric sells this tire new in pairs, which caps used value. Unchanged across the run.

upgrade.name: Utilit Seaside 20x3.0 tire
upgrade.note: The ebikesforum pick for a tougher 20x3 that fits the stock rim and fenders.

stock_parts.stock_part: Star Union mechanical disc calipers, 160mm rotors
stock_parts.comp_note: Factory spec until the May 2023 hydraulic switch (Electrek 2023-05-16). Cable-pull calipers are near worthless used, and Lectric's free retrofit kits mean many 2022-2023 bikes no longer carry them; verify what is actually on the bike, not the build date. Supersedes v1 row 'Lectric mechanical disc caliper set'.

upgrade.name: HD-E350 hydraulic disc brake set
upgrade.note: Early 3.0 owners dump the cable brakes the first time they load the bike up. Hydraulics are the standard fix.

stock_parts.stock_part: Star Union hydraulic disc brakes, 180mm rotors
stock_parts.comp_note: Production spec from May 16, 2023 onward; first sub-$1K e-bike with hydraulics (Electrek). Budget hydraulic takeoff sets list $30-60; supply comes from part-outs and lever crash damage, not upgrades. No confirmed sold set.

New replacement displays list $50-80 on eBay; used takeoffs should clear under that. No confirmed sold set. XP 3.0 kept this monochrome display for its whole run; the color TFT only arrived with the XP4.
OEM spares list $150-250 on eBay; one aftermarket 48V 10.4Ah listing showed 13 sold near $150. Health-dependent, so priced at the low end. Pack spec unchanged across the run.

upgrade.name: Long-Range 48V 14Ah battery
upgrade.note: The long-range pack is why the 10.4 comes off. Half the takeoff supply comes from range upgrades.

stock_parts.comp_note: No real used market for basic rubber grips. Bundle filler only.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Cheap grips, numb hands. GP1s are the boring right answer.

bikes.motor: 300W geared rear hub, Stealth M24 (819W peak, 28Nm)
bikes.year_notes: Launched mid-2024 as a full replacement for the 2022 XP Lite 1.0 (a separate model, not in this dossier). Shipped from day one with front+rear hydraulic 180mm brakes, the color USB-A display, and the Stealth M24 motor (Lectric product pages; Electric Bike Report review). Gates Carbon belt drive is a factory OPTION on certain editions (JW Black, Lectric Blue, etc.), not a year change. No evidence of running component changes through mid-2026, so no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Lectric's entry folder, refreshed in 2024 with hydraulic brakes and a color display. Single speed keeps it simple. Same comfort-swap pattern as the XP: smaller parts, smaller money.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same saddle family as the XP line; takeoffs list $10-25, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Same story as every Lectric: the stock saddle is the first casualty.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used. Bundle filler.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: No suspension anywhere on the Lite, so the seatpost does all the work. Standard swap.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same folding pedal as the XP line; $10-15 territory used.

upgrade.name: Chester composite platform pedals
upgrade.note: Tiny folders on a single speed you actually pedal. Riders notice fast.

stock_parts.comp_note: No real used market for basic rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Standard comfort fix on any budget commuter.

stock_parts.stock_part: 20x2.5 slick with reflective strip and Hippo Skin liner
stock_parts.comp_note: Model-specific slick with thin used demand; priced low.

upgrade.name: Big Apple 20x2.35 tire
upgrade.note: Fits the Lite's street mission and rides softer than the stock slick.

stock_parts.stock_part: Lectric color TFT display with USB-A
stock_parts.comp_note: Current-generation Lectric color display shared across the 2024+ lineup; only crash replacements create demand. No sold data found.
Sparse listings for this small pack; priced low against the $150-250 asks seen for larger Lectric packs.

upgrade.name: XP Lite Long-Range 48V 14Ah (672Wh) battery
upgrade.note: The 375Wh pack runs out fast. Long-range upgrades feed the takeoff supply.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub (1310W peak, 85Nm); Stealth M24 with torque sensor on 2.0 (2025+)
bikes.battery: 48V 14Ah (672Wh) single or dual on 1.0 (2023-2024); 48V 13Ah (624Wh) single or dual, 48V 17.5Ah long-range dual option on 2.0 (2025+)
bikes.year_notes: XPedition 1.0 launched Feb 21, 2023 (Electrek): 48V 14Ah packs, cadence sensor, 7-speed, rigid fork, XP-style backlit LCD, 20x3.0 tires. XPedition 2.0 launched early 2025 (Electric Bike Report first ride): moved DOWN to 48V 13Ah packs (17.5Ah long-range dual option), added torque sensor, Shimano Altus 8-speed, Courier 50 dual-spring 50mm suspension fork, color TFT display with USB, 20x2.5 tires, and the quieter Stealth M24 motor at the same 1310W peak. Battery, display, and tire rows are split accordingly; saddle/seatpost/grips carried over.
bikes.blurb: The budget cargo workhorse, 450 lb payload on the 2.0. Families load it up and upgrade range and rubber first. The rack is part of the frame, so takeoffs skew batteries, tires, and contact points.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Lectric saddle family; $10-25 listings, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Cargo miles are long miles. The stock saddle does not survive them.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: The 1.0 has no suspension at all, and even the 2.0's 50mm fork leaves the rear rigid. Cheapest way to make a loaded bike ride civilized.

stock_parts.comp_note: XPedition 1.0 pack (Electrek launch coverage, Feb 2023). Lectric packs list $150-250 on eBay depending on size and health; no confirmed sold set. Dual-battery owners generate takeoffs when one pack ages out. Priced low.
XPedition 2.0 pack; note it is SMALLER than the 1.0's 14Ah (Lectric product pages, EBR review). Supersedes v1 row 'Lectric XPedition 48V 13Ah battery'. No confirmed sold set; priced low.

upgrade.name: XPedition Long-Range 48V 17.5Ah battery
upgrade.note: Cargo duty eats range. Most takeoff 13Ah packs come out of dual-battery and long-range upgrades.

stock_parts.comp_note: 1.0 spec. 20-inch takeoff tires list $15-25 each; cargo wear shortens usable life, priced low. Supersedes v1 row 'Lectric XPedition 20-inch stock tire'.

upgrade.name: Pick-Up 20x2.6 cargo tire
upgrade.note: Cargo-rated casing. The stock rubber squirms once you put 200 pounds on the rack.

stock_parts.comp_note: 2.0 narrowed the tire to 20x2.5 (EBR first-ride review). Same thin used demand as other 20-inch takeoffs; priced low.

upgrade.name: Pick-Up 20x2.6 cargo tire
upgrade.note: Cargo-rated casing. The stock rubber squirms once you put 200 pounds on the rack.

stock_parts.comp_note: XPedition 1.0 used the shared XP-series monochrome display, so this row keeps the shared catalog name and merges with existing stock. Crash-replacement demand only.

stock_parts.stock_part: Lectric color TFT display with USB (2.0)
stock_parts.comp_note: Shared 2024+ Lectric color display, fitted to the XPedition from the 2.0 (2025). Crash-replacement demand only. No sold data found.
No real used market for stock rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Long hauls with a load up front make palm support worth the money.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub (1310W peak, 85Nm, Stealth M24)
bikes.year_notes: Launched Apr 16, 2024 (Electrek), shipping from June 2024, in 500W ($999) and 750W ($1,399) versions; this entry covers the 750. Shipped from launch with Star Union 602 hydraulic brakes, SR Suntour coil fork, Altus 8-speed, torque sensor, and color TFT. No evidence of mid-production component changes. Replaced by the XPress2 in May 2026 (Electrek 2026-05-19) - a separate successor model (quieter motor, XPerience torque/cadence switch); do not merge XPress2 takeoffs into these rows.
bikes.blurb: Torque-sensor commuter at a throwaway price, with an SR Suntour fork and Shimano Altus 8-speed stock. Contact-point swaps dominate; the fork and mech mostly come off when a bike gets parted out. Replaced by the XPress2 in May 2026.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Lectric saddle family; $10-25 listings, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Commuters ride daily and swap the saddle first. Same as every bike in this class.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: Front fork alone does not save your spine on city streets. The NCX finishes the job.

stock_parts.comp_note: No real used market for stock rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Daily-commute hands want the flat platform. Easy sell.

stock_parts.comp_note: Entry Suntour coil forks pull $40-70 used as takeoffs; mostly part-out supply, not upgrade swaps.
Shared 2024+ Lectric color display; crash-replacement demand only.
Downtube form factor is XPress-specific. Lectric packs list $150-250 on eBay; priced mid-low pending sold data.
Altus mechs sell $10-20 used everywhere; commodity part.
Generic 27.5 commuter takeoffs run $15-25 each used; no model-specific comps found.

upgrade.name: Super Moto-X 27.5x2.4 tire
upgrade.note: The standard e-rated street upgrade in this size. Faster and tougher than stock.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub (1310W peak, 85Nm); Stealth M24 with torque sensor on 2.0 (2025+)
bikes.year_notes: XPeak 1.0 announced Oct 27, 2023 (Electrek), shipped through 2024: 48V 14Ah (672Wh) pack, Shimano Tourney 7-speed 11-28t, 180mm rotors front and rear, monochrome 3-button display, cadence-based PWR assist, RST Renegade 80mm fork, hydraulic brakes. XPeak 2.0 announced Nov 19, 2024, shipping from Dec 30, 2024 (Electrek, 9to5Toys): 48V 15Ah (720Wh) standard / 960Wh long-range, Stealth M24 motor with torque sensor, Altus 8-speed, 203mm front rotor, color LCD with USB-C, stiffer frame. Battery and display rows split; fork, saddle, grips carried over with no evidence of change. v1's bike-level battery line (15Ah for the whole run) was wrong for 2024 bikes.
bikes.blurb: Lectric's fat tire adventure bike with an RST Renegade fork and hydraulic brakes stock. Riders who use it as intended chew through tires and want more range. Lock-on grips and real suspension mean fewer comfort swaps than the XP line.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Lectric saddle family; $10-25 listings, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Trail miles punish a budget saddle same as street miles. Still the first swap.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: Hardtail with 80mm up front still hammers the saddle. The NCX covers the back half.

stock_parts.comp_note: Lock-ons are cheap new; no meaningful used market.

upgrade.name: GA3 lock-on grips
upgrade.note: Stock lock-ons are fine. Riders who swap want the wing for washboard descents.

stock_parts.comp_note: Fat-bike coil forks pull $50-90 used; supply is part-outs, not upgrades. No confirmed sold set. No evidence the fork changed between 1.0 and 2.0, so not split.
Used 26x4 fat takeoffs list $20-35 each; heavy to ship, which drags realized prices down. Same size both generations.

upgrade.name: Minion FBF 26x4 tire
upgrade.note: The serious-traction swap. Stock knobbies are okay until the trail gets loose.

stock_parts.comp_note: XPeak 1.0 pack (Ebike Escape / EBR 1.0 reviews confirm 672Wh). v1 wrongly listed 15Ah for all years. Lectric packs list $150-250 on eBay; no XPeak-specific sold data, priced low. Cross-generation pack fitment unverified - do not sell as a 2.0 spare.
XPeak 2.0 pack (Lectric product pages; Electrek 2024-11-19). Supersedes v1 row 'Lectric XPeak 48V 15Ah battery'. Priced mid-low pending sold data.

upgrade.name: XPeak Long-Range 48V 20Ah battery
upgrade.note: Backcountry range anxiety sells the 960Wh pack and frees up the 15Ah takeoff.

stock_parts.comp_note: XPeak 1.0 shipped with a monochrome display (Ebike Escape 1.0 review); the color unit only arrived on the 2.0. v1 wrongly listed the color display for all years. Crash-replacement demand only; no sold data.

stock_parts.stock_part: Lectric backlit color LCD with USB-C
stock_parts.comp_note: Shared 2024+ Lectric color display family; the XPeak 2.0 unit carries USB-C (Lectric product page). Crash-replacement demand only.

bikes.motor: 500W mid-mounted jackshaft motor driving a rear differential (1092W peak)
bikes.year_notes: Specs revealed Jan 13, 2023, pre-orders Feb 6, 2023, shipping from May 2023 (Electrek). Verified spec: 500W motor on a central rear jackshaft driving both rear wheels through a differential (1,092W peak), 48V 14Ah (672Wh) XP-family pack, hydraulic discs plus parking brake, single speed, 65 lb folding frame. No evidence of mid-production component changes across the run, so no year splits. Replaced by the XP Trike2 (750W, 2025) - a separate model; do not merge Trike2 takeoffs here.
bikes.blurb: The default budget electric trike until the Trike2 replaced it in 2025. Buyers skew older and want backrest saddles almost on day one. Hydraulic brakes, single speed, and the XP-pattern battery keep parts interchangeable and moving.

stock_parts.comp_note: Wide takeoff saddles list $10-25; steady trickle of trike-owner demand, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Tri-Cruiser saddle with backrest
upgrade.note: Trike buyers want a backrest, full stop. This is the swap nearly every mobility-minded owner makes.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same pack family as the XP long-range, which broadens the buyer pool. OEM asks run $200-280; priced under.
Shared XP-series display; crash and water damage drive replacement demand.
Low-speed trike duty means slow wear and thin swap volume; priced low.
Bolt-on baskets hold value with trike buyers who removed theirs; thin data, priced low.
No real used market for stock rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Arthritic hands are half this bike's market. Ergo grips are an easy add-on sale.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same folding pedal as the XP line; $10-15 territory used.

bikes.motor: 750W Bafang geared rear hub (80Nm), torque + cadence sensors
bikes.battery: 52V 19.2Ah (998Wh) pack; case style changed G1 to G2 mid-run
bikes.year_notes: Reviewed from 2019 (TheNextWeb, T3) with the same core spec it kept for its whole run: 750W Bafang hub, 52V 19.2Ah pack, Tektro HD-E350 hydraulic brakes, torque+cadence sensing. A step-through variant was added Aug 2021 (Electrek 2021-08-10). The 52V pack CASE changed from G1 to G2 style mid-run (grip handle, LED meter, AirTag bay), but Juiced sold the G2 as backwards compatible with every 52V Juiced bike, so no fitment-driven year split is warranted; identify packs by case style when listing. Juiced collapsed into bankruptcy/auction in late 2024. No verified mid-production component change beyond the pack case, so no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Fast 52V commuter from a brand that collapsed into bankruptcy in 2024. Owners cannot buy OEM anything anymore, so working takeoffs, above all batteries, displays, and controllers, carry real scarcity value. Price and hold accordingly.

stock_parts.comp_note: Name-brand takeoff saddles pull $15-30 used; better than house-brand but still no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: The Selle Royal is decent, but 32 mph commuters still trade it for more padding.

stock_parts.stock_part: 52V 19.2Ah pack (G1 case early, G2 case later)
stock_parts.comp_note: New-replacement listings run $520-620 since the 2024 shutdown; working used G2 packs list $250-450. Demand is real, confirmed solds are thin, so priced at the low end. Same 52V 19.2Ah electrical spec all years; G1-case packs (earlier bikes) rebuild via ChiBatterySystems/GreenBikeKit, and the G2 case is backwards compatible with every 52V Juiced bike - note case style in the listing, no year split needed.

upgrade.name: G2 52V replacement pack
upgrade.note: Juiced is gone, so a dying pack means aftermarket or rebuild. Working takeoff packs move fast because of it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Aftermarket CrossCurrent/RipCurrent replacements run about $90 new at GreenBikeKit; used OEM priced under that. Dead brand keeps demand alive. CCX was 52V for its entire run.

upgrade.name: KT 52V controller (CrossCurrent/RipCurrent fit)
upgrade.note: When the stock controller cooks there is no Juiced to call. The KT swap is the known path.

stock_parts.comp_note: No OEM source since the shutdown; scarce takeoffs command real money from stranded owners, but no sold set found. Same matrix display family across the run.

stock_parts.stock_part: Bafang 750W geared rear hub laced to 27.5 wheel
stock_parts.comp_note: Bafang hub motor wheels list $120-250 on eBay new and used; laced takeoffs priced at the low end pending sold data.
Entry suspension forks pull $40-70 used; part-out supply only.
Common size with plentiful new alternatives; used takeoffs priced low.

upgrade.name: Super Moto-X 27.5x2.4 tire
upgrade.note: E-rated casing built for exactly this kind of 32 mph commuter abuse.

stock_parts.stock_part: Juiced rear rack and fender set
stock_parts.catalog_name: Juiced rear rack and fender set (CrossCurrent)
stock_parts.comp_note: Dead brand makes even bolt-on tin worth something to owners replacing bent parts; thin data, priced low.

bikes.motor: 750W Bafang geared rear hub (80Nm; ~1,036W peak on 2018 spec, ~1,300W peak quoted on later 52V bikes)
bikes.battery: 48V 13Ah (624Wh) standard on early bikes (2018-2019, 52V optional); 52V 19.2Ah (998Wh) standard on later bikes (~2020-2024)
bikes.year_notes: EBR's archived March 2018 review documents the original spec: 48V 13Ah LG 18650 pack, Bafang 750W hub (1,036W peak), Star Union 150PYD HYDRAULIC discs on 180mm rotors, MoZo 80mm air fork, 9-speed drivetrain, Kenda Krusade Sport 26x4 tires, Liandian monochrome matrix LCD. 52V packs were optional then; owners on the EBR forum confirm the 48V controller will NOT power a 52V pack ('It fit okay but the bike would not power up at all'), so 48V and 52V bikes have incompatible battery+controller sets. 52V 19.2Ah became the standard spec on later bikes; the exact 2019/2020 changeover is not firmly documented, so the ~2020 boundary below is estimated - identify bikes by the voltage printed on pack and controller, not by year alone. Later bikes also moved to Tektro HD-E350 hydraulic brakes (Juiced sold the HD-E350 as the RipCurrent part; reviews call it an upgrade from the earlier Star Union units) and CST BFT tires. Brakes were hydraulic in ALL years - only the brand changed - so no brake row split. Pack case went G1 to G2 mid-run on 52V bikes; G2 is backwards compatible with every 52V Juiced bike.
bikes.blurb: Juiced's best seller: a 28 mph fat tire commuter with a Shimano 9-speed and hydraulic brakes. The brand's 2024 collapse turned every working takeoff into supported-life insurance for stranded owners. Batteries and controllers are the money parts.

stock_parts.comp_note: Name-brand takeoff saddles pull $15-30 used; EBR forum threads document owners swapping this one out.

upgrade.name: A.R.S. Spring saddle
upgrade.note: Owners on the EBR forum call the difference light years. The stock gel bottoms out under bigger riders.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used; bundle filler.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: Pairs with the saddle swap. Fat tires alone do not save your back at 28 mph.

stock_parts.comp_note: Early 48V bikes only. EBR forum confirms the 48V controller rejects 52V packs, so stranded gen-1 owners need exactly this part to stay on their original battery - real scarcity, tiny buyer pool. No sold data; year boundary estimated (~2020 changeover).

Standard on later 52V bikes (boundary estimated ~2020; verify the voltage printed on the unit). Juiced sold an official upgraded controller for this bike and forum owners installed it, creating stock takeoffs. Aftermarket replacements run about $90 new; used OEM priced under.

upgrade.name: KT 52V controller (CrossCurrent/RipCurrent fit)
upgrade.note: The factory hot-rod controller died with the company. The KT is the only game left.

stock_parts.comp_note: Original standard pack per EBR's March 2018 review; v1 wrongly listed the 52V G2 for all years. 52V was optional in this era, so some early bikes carry 52V packs from the factory. Dead-brand scarcity is real but the 48V buyer pool is small and health risk is high on 2018-2019 cells; priced low, no sold set. Year boundary estimated.

stock_parts.stock_part: 52V 19.2Ah pack (G1 case early, G2 case later)
stock_parts.comp_note: Standard on later bikes (boundary estimated ~2020; also fits optioned-up earlier 52V bikes). Same pack as the CrossCurrent X. New replacements $520-620, used working packs listed $250-450; thin confirmed solds, priced low. G2 case is backwards compatible with every 52V Juiced bike.

upgrade.name: G2 52V replacement pack
upgrade.note: Dead brand plus aging cells equals the strongest demand line in the whole Juiced catalog.

stock_parts.stock_part: Bafang 750W geared rear hub laced to 26x4 wheel
stock_parts.comp_note: Same Bafang 750W hub across the run (peak-watt quotes rose with the 52V controller, not a different motor). EBR forum threads show owners swapping this hub for bigger motors, so takeoffs exist. Bafang hub wheels list $120-250; priced at the low end.
Original fitment per EBR's March 2018 review; later bikes shipped CST BFT. Krusades still sell new, capping used value; most of these are worn out by now anyway. Year boundary estimated.

upgrade.name: Supercell 26x4 street tire
upgrade.note: Commuters ditch the knobbies for slicks and pick up real range doing it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Later-production fitment (boundary estimated). BFTs run $40-50 new; used takeoffs realistically $15-25 given shipping weight.

upgrade.name: Supercell 26x4 street tire
upgrade.note: Commuters ditch the knobbies for slicks and pick up real range doing it.

stock_parts.stock_part: Adjustable air suspension fork with lockout
stock_parts.comp_note: 2018 spec sheet names a MoZo 80mm air fork; later spec sheets just say adjustable air fork with lockout. No dated evidence of a distinct fork change, so not split - note the brand stamped on the lowers when listing. Fat-bike air forks pull $50-90 used; no sold set.
Same Liandian-made matrix display family across the run and across the Juiced lineup; unobtainable new since the shutdown. Scarcity value, thin data.

bikes.year_notes: Launched April 2021 (9to5Toys 2021-04-21; Electrek review 2021-10-21): 750W nominal RetroBlade motor with claimed 1.8kW peak, 52V 15Ah (780Wh) pack (upsized from the base Scorpion's 13Ah/676Wh), 20x4.25 tires, hydraulic 180mm discs, adjustable hydraulic fork, Shimano 8-speed. v1 corrections: battery is 15Ah not 15.6Ah (15Ah x 52V = 780Wh exactly; the 15.6Ah figure comes from aftermarket rebuild listings), motor is Juiced's RetroBlade unit not a generic Bafang, and production ended around Sept 2023 when the 1,000W Scorpion X2 superseded it (Electrek 2023-09-06) - not 2024. No evidence of running changes within the X's own production, so no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Full-suspension 52V moped, orphaned when Juiced folded in 2024. Almost nothing on it cross-shops with standard bike parts, which cuts both ways: takeoffs are scarce and wanted, but the only buyers are other Scorpion owners.

stock_parts.comp_note: Corrected from v1's '52V 15.6Ah' (OEM spec is 15Ah/780Wh per Electrek's 2021 review; 15.6Ah is the aftermarket rebuild capacity from YosePower and others - rename any DB row carrying the old name). Aftermarket replacements list $520-590 on eBay since the shutdown; used OEM packs priced at half or less on health risk. No confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: 52V G-pack rebuild service
upgrade.note: Chi rebuilds the dead pack in the original case. The only sane route since Juiced folded.

stock_parts.comp_note: Model-specific moped seat with no aftermarket equal; dead-brand scarcity supports the price, but data is thin.
Odd moped size still sold new around $50-60; heavy throttle bikes wear tires fast so demand recurs. Used priced low.

stock_parts.stock_part: Triple-clamp hydraulic fork with adjustable settings
stock_parts.comp_note: Moped triple-trees have no drop-in substitute and the brand is dead; takeoffs hold value but no sold set exists.
Generic coil-over pairs sell $30-60 used; Scorpion fitment adds a little, thin data.

upgrade.name: RCP-2S coil shocks (pair)
upgrade.note: Standard e-moped shock upgrade when the stock coils get mushy under a passenger.

stock_parts.stock_part: Juiced LCD advanced matrix display with USB
stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Juiced display, unobtainable new; scarcity value from stranded owners, no sold set.
Higher-amp unit than the CrossCurrent/RipCurrent controller; same dead-brand dynamics, priced in line with aftermarket 52V replacements near $90 new.
